title: Understanding the System
text: The system consists of a rotating disk and a bug. Initially, the bug is at the edge of the disk, and then it moves to the center. How does this affect the system's moment of inertia?

title: Conservation of Angular Momentum
text: Since there are no external torques acting on the system, the angular momentum of the system is conserved. What is the initial angular momentum of the system?

title: Initial Moment of Inertia
text: The initial moment of inertia of the system is the sum of the moment of inertia of the disk and the bug. Calculate the initial moment of inertia.

title: Final Moment of Inertia
text: When the bug moves to the center, its contribution to the moment of inertia becomes zero. What is the final moment of inertia of the system?

title: Final Angular Velocity
text: Using the conservation of angular momentum, calculate the final angular velocity of the system.

title: Calculate New Kinetic Energy
text: The kinetic energy of a rotating system is given by $$\frac{1}{2} I {\omega}^2$$. Use the final moment of inertia and angular velocity to find the new kinetic energy.
